options.text:
Before proceeding, ensure you have already created:

✓ Azure OpenAI resource with deployed model
✓ Azure Key Vault
✓ Azure Cache for Redis
✓ Azure Storage Account with Files share
✓ Azure AD App Registration (instructions below)

This deployment connects to your existing resources - it does NOT create them.

SmartLib uses Azure Active Directory to authenticate users. You must create an App Registration in YOUR Azure AD tenant before deployment.

Quick Setup: Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration → Name: 'SmartLib' → Single tenant → Create

After creation: Certificates & secrets → New client secret → Copy the VALUE (not Secret ID)

For detailed step-by-step instructions with screenshots, visit the prerequisites page.

elements.name: tenantId
elements.label: Azure AD Tenant ID
elements.toolTip: Your organization's Azure Active Directory tenant ID (GUID). Find in Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → Overview → Tenant ID.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid tenant ID GUID (format: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx)

options.text: How to find: Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → Overview → Tenant ID (copy the GUID)

elements.name: APP_CLIENT_ID
elements.label: Application (Client) ID
elements.toolTip: The Application (client) ID from your Azure AD App Registration. Find in the app registration's Overview page.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid client ID GUID (format: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx)

options.text: How to find: Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → App registrations → Your SmartLib app → Overview → Application (client) ID

elements.name: APP_CLIENT_SECRET
elements.toolTip: The client secret VALUE (not the Secret ID) from your Azure AD App Registration. This is shown ONLY ONCE when you create it.

constraints.validationMessage: Client secret must be at least 8 characters

options.text: How to create: Azure AD → App registrations → Your app → Certificates & secrets → New client secret → Copy VALUE immediately (not Secret ID). You cannot retrieve this later!
IMPORTANT: After deployment, update your Azure AD App Registration's Redirect URI with the actual web app URL from deployment outputs: https://{webapp-name}.azurewebsites.net/auth/callback

🏢 ENTERPRISE TIER

SmartLib Enterprise uses PostgreSQL with pgvector extension for scalable vector search. This tier is designed for 10+ concurrent users with production workloads.

⚠️ CRITICAL PREREQUISITE

You MUST create PostgreSQL Flexible Server with pgvector extension BEFORE deploying SmartLib Enterprise.

✅ Required Setup:
1. Create PostgreSQL Flexible Server (version 15 or 16)
2. Create a database for SmartLib
3. Enable pgvector extension on the database
4. Configure firewall to allow Azure services

Without pgvector extension, deployment will FAIL!

📋 HOW TO ENABLE PGVECTOR EXTENSION:

1. Azure Portal → PostgreSQL Flexible Server → Your server
2. Settings → Server parameters
3. Search for 'azure.extensions'
4. Add 'vector' to the allowed list
5. Click 'Save' and restart server

6. Connect to your database using psql or Azure Cloud Shell:
   psql -h YOURSERVER.postgres.database.azure.com -U USERNAME -d DATABASENAME

7. Run this command:
   CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

8. Verify installation:
   \dx vector
   (You should see 'vector' in the extensions list)

⚠️ You must complete this BEFORE proceeding with deployment!

elements.name: postgresServerSelector
elements.label: Select PostgreSQL Flexible Server
elements.toolTip: Select your existing PostgreSQL Flexible Server with pgvector extension enabled.

options.text:
[concat('✅ PostgreSQL Server selected
📍 Server: ', steps('databaseStep').postgresServerSelector.name, '
📍 Location: ', steps('databaseStep').postgresServerSelector.location, '
📍 Version: ', steps('databaseStep').postgresServerSelector.properties.version, '
📍 Resource Group: ', first(split(last(split(steps('databaseStep').postgresServerSelector.id, '/resourceGroups/')), '/providers/')), '
🔗 FQDN: ', steps('databaseStep').postgresServerSelector.properties.fullyQualifiedDomainName)]

⚠️ POSTGRESQL VERSION WARNING

Your selected PostgreSQL server is using an older version. SmartLib Enterprise recommends PostgreSQL 15 or 16 for best pgvector performance and compatibility.

Consider upgrading your PostgreSQL server version.

elements.name: postgresDatabaseName
elements.label: Database name
elements.toolTip: Name of the database within your PostgreSQL server. This database must already exist with pgvector extension enabled.

constraints.validationMessage: Database name must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores (max 63 characters)

options.text:
📝 How to create database:

1. Azure Portal → PostgreSQL Flexible Server → Your server
2. Click 'Databases' in left sidebar
3. Click '+ Add database'
4. Enter database name (e.g., 'smartlibdb')
5. Click 'Save'
6. Enable pgvector extension (see instructions above)
7. Copy the database name and paste it in the field above

elements.name: postgresAdminUser
elements.label: PostgreSQL username
elements.toolTip: Database user with permissions to create tables, indexes, and use pgvector extension. Can be the server admin or a dedicated SmartLib user.

constraints.validationMessage: Username must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores (max 63 characters)

options.text:
🔐 Required database permissions for this user:
• CREATE tables
• CREATE indexes
• INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, SELECT on tables
• USAGE on vector extension

The server admin user has all these permissions by default.

elements.name: postgresPasswordOption
elements.label: How to provide password
elements.toolTip: Choose whether to enter the password directly or reference a Key Vault secret (recommended for production)

elements.name: postgresPasswordSecretUri
elements.label: PostgreSQL password secret URI
elements.toolTip: Key Vault secret URI containing the PostgreSQL user password. This is the recommended secure method.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid Azure Key Vault secret URI (format: https://{vault}.vault.azure.net/secrets/{secret-name})

options.text:
📋 How to store PostgreSQL password in Key Vault:

1. Azure Portal → Your Key Vault
2. Secrets → + Generate/Import
3. Name: POSTGRES-PASSWORD (or any name you prefer)
4. Value: Your PostgreSQL user password
5. Click 'Create'
6. Click on the secret name
7. Click on the current version
8. Copy 'Secret Identifier' (full URL)
9. Paste it in the field above

Example URI:
https://your-keyvault.vault.azure.net/secrets/POSTGRES-PASSWORD/abc123

elements.name: postgresAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Password for the PostgreSQL user. For production deployments, use Key Vault secret URI instead.

constraints.validationMessage: Password must be at least 8 characters

options.text:
⚠️ SECURITY WARNING

Storing passwords directly in deployment parameters is not recommended for production environments. Consider using Key Vault secret URI instead for better security.

⚠️ FINAL CHECKLIST - VERIFY BEFORE DEPLOYING:

✅ PostgreSQL Flexible Server created (version 15 or 16 recommended)
✅ Database created in PostgreSQL server
✅ pgvector extension enabled on the database
   Run: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
   Verify: \dx vector
✅ Firewall configured to allow Azure services
   Portal → PostgreSQL → Networking → Check 'Allow Azure services'
✅ Database user has CREATE permissions

❌ Missing any of these? Deployment will FAIL!

💡 Test your connection first using psql or Azure Data Studio before deploying.

🔐 NETWORK ACCESS CONFIGURATION

Your PostgreSQL server firewall must allow:
• Azure services access (REQUIRED for App Service connectivity)
• Your IP address (for initial setup and testing)

How to configure:
1. Azure Portal → PostgreSQL Flexible Server → Networking
2. Check 'Allow public access from any Azure service within Azure'
3. Add your current IP for management access
4. Click 'Save'

⚠️ Without Azure services access, SmartLib cannot connect to your database!

🔒 SSL/TLS CONNECTION

SmartLib Enterprise connects to PostgreSQL using SSL mode 'require' for secure communication. Ensure your PostgreSQL server has SSL enabled (default for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server).

subLabel.preValidation: Configure Key Vault, Redis, and Storage services

elements.name: keyVaultSelector
elements.label: Select Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Select your existing Azure Key Vault resource from the dropdown. SmartLib will use this to store secrets.

options.text:
[concat('✅ Key Vault selected
📍 Name: ', steps('infrastructureStep').keyVaultSelector.name, '
📍 Location: ', steps('infrastructureStep').keyVaultSelector.location, '
📍 Resource Group: ', last(split(steps('infrastructureStep').keyVaultSelector.id, '/resourceGroups/')), '
🔗 Vault URI: ', steps('infrastructureStep').keyVaultSelector.properties.vaultUri)]

elements.name: existingKeyVaultName
elements.label: Key Vault name (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Key Vault name from selected resource

elements.name: existingKeyVaultResourceGroup
elements.label: Key Vault resource group (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Resource group from selected Key Vault

options.text: Select your Key Vault from the dropdown above. The name and resource group will be automatically populated.

✅ SINGLE-STEP DEPLOYMENT: SmartLib now deploys in one step with automatic configuration.

🔐 Storage mount uses direct storage key (auto-fetched)
🔑 App secrets use Key Vault references (with auto-retry)

No manual intervention required after deployment!

elements.name: createRoleAssignment
elements.label: Attempt automatic Key Vault role assignments (may fail for cross-RG deployments)
elements.toolTip: Automatic role assignment creation may fail if Key Vault is in a different resource group. If disabled or fails, create role assignments manually after deployment.

options.text:
⚠️ MANUAL ROLE ASSIGNMENT REQUIRED

After deployment completes, grant Key Vault access to the app identities:

1. Copy the az commands from deployment outputs
2. Run both commands to create role assignments
3. Wait 5-10 minutes for Azure RBAC propagation
4. Restart both web and worker apps

Note: Key Vault references will show red/error until role assignments complete and propagate.

⚠️ CROSS-RESOURCE-GROUP DEPLOYMENT DETECTED

Key Vault is in a different resource group. Ensure you have 'Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write' permission on the Key Vault resource group.

If deployment fails with authorization error, you can:
1. Deploy without role assignments (uncheck above)
2. Manually add role assignments after deployment
3. Request permissions from your Azure admin

elements.name: redisConnectionString
elements.label: Redis connection string
elements.toolTip: Redis connection string in format: rediss://:password@hostname:6380?ssl_cert_reqs=none. Required if Secret URI not provided.

options.text:
⚠️ IMPORTANT: You must modify Azure's default connection string!

How to get Redis connection string:

1. Azure Portal → Azure Cache for Redis → Your Redis Cache
2. Click 'Access keys' (left sidebar)
3. Copy 'Primary connection string (StackExchange.Redis)'

4. MODIFY THE FORMAT (Azure's format won't work):

   Azure gives you:
   smartlib-redis.redis.cache.windows.net:6380,password=[YOUR_PASSWORD],ssl=True,abortConnect=False

   You MUST change to:
   rediss://:[YOUR_PASSWORD]@smartlib-redis.redis.cache.windows.net:6380?ssl_cert_reqs=none

   Pattern: rediss://:PASSWORD@HOSTNAME:6380?ssl_cert_reqs=none

⚠️ Don't forget: ?ssl_cert_reqs=none at the end!

💡 RECOMMENDED: Store in Key Vault (see Secret URI field below)

elements.name: redisConnectionStringSecretUri
elements.label: Redis connection string secret URI (RECOMMENDED)
elements.toolTip: Key Vault secret URI containing Redis connection string. This is more secure than providing the string directly above.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid Azure Key Vault secret URI

options.text: ⚠️ WARNING: You must provide either a Redis connection string or a Key Vault secret URI containing the connection string.

How to store Redis connection in Key Vault (RECOMMENDED):

1. Get connection string from Azure (see above)
2. Convert to SmartLib format (see example above)
   ⚠️ Make sure it ends with: ?ssl_cert_reqs=none

3. Azure Portal → Key Vault → Secrets → + Generate/Import
4. Name: REDIS-CONNECTION-STRING
5. Value: rediss://:PASSWORD@HOSTNAME:6380?ssl_cert_reqs=none
6. Click 'Create'
7. Click on the secret name
8. Copy 'Secret Identifier' (full URL)
9. Paste here

Example Secret URI:
https://your-keyvault.vault.azure.net/secrets/REDIS-CONNECTION-STRING/abc123def456

elements.name: storageAccountSelector
elements.label: Select Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Select your existing Azure Storage Account from the dropdown. SmartLib will mount an Azure Files share from this account.

options.text:
[concat('✅ Storage Account selected
📍 Name: ', steps('storageServicesStep').storageAccountSelector.name, '
📍 Location: ', steps('storageServicesStep').storageAccountSelector.location, '
📍 Resource Group: ', last(split(steps('storageServicesStep').storageAccountSelector.id, '/resourceGroups/')), '
📦 SKU: ', steps('storageServicesStep').storageAccountSelector.sku.name, '
🔒 Primary File Endpoint: ', steps('storageServicesStep').storageAccountSelector.properties.primaryEndpoints.file)]

elements.name: storageAccountName
elements.label: Storage account name (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Storage account name from selected resource

elements.name: storageAccountResourceGroup
elements.label: Storage account resource group (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Resource group from selected Storage Account

options.text: Select your Storage Account from the dropdown above. The name and resource group will be automatically populated.

elements.name: dataShareName
elements.label: Azure Files share name
elements.toolTip: Name of the Azure Files share mounted at /home/data inside the containers.

options.text: How to find: Azure Portal → Storage accounts → Your Storage Account → File shares → Copy the file share name

elements.name: storageAccountKey
elements.toolTip: Access key for the storage account. Required for mounting Azure Files share. Copy from: Azure Portal → Storage Account → Access keys → key1 → Copy

constraints.validationMessage: Storage account key must be at least 44 characters (Base64 encoded)

options.text:
📋 How to get your storage account access key:

1. Azure Portal → Storage accounts → Your Storage Account
2. Click 'Access keys' in the left sidebar
3. Under 'key1', click 'Show' button
4. Click the copy icon next to the key value
5. Paste the key in the field above

🔐 The key is used to mount the Azure Files share and is stored securely.

elements.name: createStorageRoleAssignment
elements.label: Grant identity-based storage access (optional)
elements.toolTip: Creates managed identity role assignments for Storage Blob Data Contributor. Enable if you plan to use blob storage features.

options.text: ℹ️ Identity-based access is for additional storage features (blobs, queues). The storage key is still required for mounting Azure Files share.

subLabel.preValidation: Configure Azure OpenAI and Document Intelligence

options.text:
[concat('✅ Azure OpenAI resource selected
📍 Name: ', steps('aiServicesStep').openAIResourceSelector.name, '
📍 Location: ', steps('aiServicesStep').openAIResourceSelector.location, '
🔗 Endpoint: ', steps('aiServicesStep').openAIResourceSelector.properties.endpoint)]

elements.name: openAIAzureName
elements.label: Azure OpenAI resource name (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Resource name from selected Azure OpenAI resource

elements.name: azureOpenAIEndpointComputed
elements.label: Azure OpenAI Endpoint (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Endpoint automatically populated from the selected Azure OpenAI resource.

options.text: Select your Azure OpenAI resource from the dropdown above. The endpoint will be automatically populated.

elements.name: azureOpenAIDeployment
elements.label: Chat model deployment name
elements.toolTip: The deployment name for your chat/completion model in Azure OpenAI Studio. This is the name YOU chose when creating the deployment, not the base model name.

constraints.validationMessage: Deployment name must be 1-64 characters, start and end with alphanumeric, and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, and periods.

options.text:
⚠️ PREREQUISITE: You must deploy 'text-embedding-3-small' model in Azure OpenAI Studio BEFORE proceeding.

Why? SmartLib is pre-configured to work with this specific embedding model for document search.

elements.name: azureEmbeddingDeployment
elements.label: Embedding model deployment name
elements.toolTip: The CUSTOM NAME you gave when deploying the embedding model in Azure OpenAI Studio. This can be any name you chose.

constraints.validationMessage: Deployment name must be 1-64 characters, start and end with alphanumeric, and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, and periods.

options.text:
📍 How to create the embedding deployment:

1. Go to Azure OpenAI Studio (https://oai.azure.com)
2. Select your resource → Deployments → Create new deployment
3. Model: Select 'text-embedding-3-small'
4. Deployment name: Enter YOUR custom name (e.g., 'smartlib-embedding')
5. Click 'Create'
6. Copy YOUR deployment name and paste it above

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Enter the DEPLOYMENT NAME you created (can be anything), NOT 'text-embedding-3-small'

elements.name: azureOpenAIAPIVersion
elements.label: Azure OpenAI API version
elements.toolTip: API version string for Azure OpenAI requests.

elements.name: defaultEmbeddingModel
elements.label: Base embedding model identifier
elements.toolTip: The base OpenAI model name (not your deployment name). SmartLib is pre-configured for 'text-embedding-3-small'.

options.text:
ℹ️ This must match the base model you deployed in Azure OpenAI Studio.

✅ If you deployed 'text-embedding-3-small' → Keep default value
⚠️ If you deployed a different model → Update this field to match

Supported models:
• text-embedding-3-small (Recommended - pre-configured)
• text-embedding-3-large
• text-embedding-ada-002

📋 SUMMARY - Understanding the difference:

1️⃣ DEPLOYMENT NAME (field above 'Embedding model deployment name'):
   • YOUR custom name when you created the deployment
   • Examples: 'my-embedding', 'smartlib-embed', 'prod-embedding'
   • Can be ANYTHING you want

2️⃣ BASE MODEL IDENTIFIER (field above 'Base embedding model identifier'):
   • The actual OpenAI model name
   • Must be: 'text-embedding-3-small' (recommended)
   • SmartLib uses this to know which model you're using

Example:
• You deployed model: 'text-embedding-3-small'
• You named your deployment: 'my-company-embedding'
• Deployment name field: 'my-company-embedding' ← YOUR name
• Model identifier field: 'text-embedding-3-small' ← OpenAI's model name

elements.name: azureOpenAIKey
elements.label: Azure OpenAI key
elements.toolTip: API key for Azure OpenAI. Leave blank when using a Key Vault secret URI.

options.text: How to find: Azure Portal → Azure OpenAI → Your OpenAI Resource → Keys and Endpoint → Copy Key 1 or Key 2

elements.name: azureOpenAIKeySecretUri
elements.label: Azure OpenAI key secret URI
elements.toolTip: Key Vault secret URI containing the Azure OpenAI key (optional).

options.text:
📄 Document Intelligence is OPTIONAL

If enabled, SmartLib uses Azure Document Intelligence for advanced document parsing (tables, forms, complex layouts).

If disabled, SmartLib uses built-in OCR for basic document processing.

elements.name: useDocIntelligence
elements.label: Enable Azure Document Intelligence (optional)
elements.toolTip: Check this box if you want to use Azure Document Intelligence for enhanced document processing capabilities.

options.text:
[concat('✅ Document Intelligence resource selected
📍 Name: ', steps('aiServicesStep').docIntelligenceSelector.name, '
📍 Location: ', steps('aiServicesStep').docIntelligenceSelector.location, '
🔗 Endpoint: ', steps('aiServicesStep').docIntelligenceSelector.properties.endpoint)]

elements.name: docIntelligenceEndpoint
elements.label: Document Intelligence endpoint (auto-detected)
elements.toolTip: Endpoint URL automatically populated from the selected resource.

options.text:
💡 IMPORTANT: Store your Document Intelligence API key in Key Vault and provide the secret URI below.

How to get the key:
1. Azure Portal → Document Intelligence resource
2. Keys and Endpoint
3. Copy Key 1 or Key 2
4. Store in Key Vault as a secret
5. Paste the secret URI here

elements.name: docIntelligenceKeySecretUri
elements.label: Document Intelligence key secret URI
elements.toolTip: Key Vault secret URI containing the Azure Document Intelligence API key.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid Azure Key Vault secret URI (format: https://{vault}.vault.azure.net/secrets/{secret-name})

subLabel.preValidation: Configure container registry and deployment options

options.text: ℹ️ SmartLib resources will be deployed to the same region as your resource group for optimal performance and cost efficiency.

SmartLib creates three main resources:
• App Service Plan: {prefix}-plan
• Web Application: {prefix}-web
• Worker Application: {prefix}-worker

Choose a unique prefix to avoid naming conflicts.

elements.name: appPrefix
elements.label: Resource name prefix
elements.toolTip: Unique prefix for resource naming. Must be unique within your Azure subscription. Resources will be named: {prefix}-web, {prefix}-worker, {prefix}-plan

constraints.validationMessage: Prefix must be 3-40 characters, start/end with lowercase alphanumeric, contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.

validations.message: Resource names conflict with existing App Services. Choose a different prefix to avoid deployment errors.

options.text:
[concat('✅ Resource names are available:
• Web App: ', steps('deploymentStep').appPrefix, '-web
• Worker App: ', steps('deploymentStep').appPrefix, '-worker')]

[concat('❌ DEPLOYMENT WILL FAIL - Resource name conflicts detected:

', if(not(steps('deploymentStep').webAppAvailabilityApi.nameAvailable), concat('• Web App: ', steps('deploymentStep').appPrefix, '-web (Already exists)
'), ''), if(not(steps('deploymentStep').workerAppAvailabilityApi.nameAvailable), concat('• Worker App: ', steps('deploymentStep').appPrefix, '-worker (Already exists)'), ''), '

⚠️ Choose a different prefix to proceed.')]

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Choose a unique prefix to prevent deployment conflicts.

✅ Good examples:
• smartlib-acme
• smartlib-contoso
• smartlib-dev-001

❌ Avoid:
• smartlib-basic (default, may conflict)
• Names with uppercase or special characters
• Names over 40 characters

💡 TIP: Use your company name or project identifier
🔄 REDEPLOYMENT: Using the same prefix will UPDATE existing resources instead of creating new ones. This ensures idempotent deployments and prevents duplicate resources.

elements.name: appServicePlanSkuName
elements.label: App Service plan SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the pricing tier for the SmartLib App Service plan.

options.text:
Recommended SKUs:
• B1: Development/testing (~$13/month)
• S1: Small production workloads (~$70/month)
• P1v2: Production with high availability (~$146/month)

elements.name: enableAutomaticUpdates
elements.label: Enable automatic updates
elements.toolTip: Automatically pull latest container images from SmartLib registry. When disabled, you control when to update via Azure Portal Deployment Center.

options.text: When enabled, your SmartLib installation will automatically check for and pull the latest container images from the publisher's registry. When disabled, you can manually sync updates via Azure Portal → App Service → Deployment Center → Sync button.

elements.name: autoPromoteAdmin
elements.label: Auto-promote admin account
elements.toolTip: Bootstrap the initial SmartLib admin account on first startup.

elements.name: runDefaultModels
elements.label: Load default models during deployment
elements.toolTip: Run create_default_models.py automatically when the web app starts.

options.text: This creates the initial SmartLib administrator account. After deployment, you can create additional users and change the admin password through the web interface.

elements.name: appAdminUsername
elements.label: Admin username (locked)
elements.toolTip: Admin username is locked to 'admin' for consistency. You can create additional admin users with different names after deployment.

constraints.validationMessage: Admin username must be 'admin' and cannot be changed during deployment.

options.text:
Admin password requirements:
• Minimum 8 characters
• At least 1 uppercase letter (A-Z)
• At least 1 lowercase letter (a-z)
• At least 1 digit (0-9)
• At least 1 special character (@$!%*?&#)

elements.name: appAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Initial password for the SmartLib admin account. Must meet the requirements above.

constraints.validationMessage: Password must be at least 8 characters and contain: uppercase letter, lowercase letter, digit, and special character (@$!%*?&#)

elements.name: appAdminPasswordSecretUri
elements.label: Admin password secret URI
elements.toolTip: Optional Key Vault secret URI containing the admin account password.

elements.name: appAdminEmail
elements.label: Admin email
elements.toolTip: Contact email for the SmartLib admin account. Required for admin account creation.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email address (e.g., admin@example.com)

elements.name: appAdminEmailSecretUri
elements.label: Admin email secret URI
elements.toolTip: Optional Key Vault secret URI containing the admin email address.
